title: Best-path never names or reasons the O-1A-vs-O-1B trade-off for a hybrid creative; keyword mock scores O-1B worst and recommends EB-1A — steering an arts-eligible game director away from her real choice
expected: For a game director whose path is genuinely ambiguous, the best-path read names the O-1A-vs-O-1B choice and reasons it from her hybrid evidence: tech/GDC talk -> O-1A Original contribution; game design + 250k Steam units + Overwhelmingly Positive + IGN/Polygon -> O-1B Lead role + Record of major commercial/critical success + Reviews & press. The recommendation should surface that trade-off, not silently default.
got: recommendBestPath is fully deterministic — scoreProgram runs the keyword mock (mockQualification) for every program (best-path.ts:71-98) and rationaleFor emits a mechanical 'You clear X (n/criteria, needs 3)' line (best-path.ts:120-135) that never reasons sciences-vs-arts. Simulated on Yuki's real wording (regexes verbatim from packs.ts): O-1A 3/8, EB-1A 3/10, O-1B 1/6 -> ranked EB-1A > O-1A > O-1B, recommends EB-1A. Her commercial-success story (Steam sales/units) matches NOTHING in the O-1A pack; her IGN/Polygon press fails the O-1A PRESS regex; the O-1B pack — arguably her strongest — scores last because its regexes miss Steam/units/Overwhelmingly Positive/IGN/Polygon (packs.ts:104-139). The ambiguity she came to resolve is hidden behind a keyword tally.
suggested_acceptance: Best-path recommendation, on a hybrid arts-tech (game-director) record, explicitly names the O-1A-vs-O-1B choice and gives a reasoned trade-off (which evidence argues sciences vs arts), rather than a single mechanical margin line; O-1B is not structurally buried for a director whose commercial/critical success is its strongest signal.
l2_priority: Run the live best-path on Yuki's real paste and assert the recommendation text NAMES O-1A-vs-O-1B and reasons it from her hybrid evidence (tech->Original-contribution, game->Lead-role+commercial-success); confirm O-1B is not ranked last for a game director with documented sales+reviews. Related: OA-QV-01, lf-qual-02.

title: Best-path runs the keyword mock with no model path at all even in the live env; all three programs share threshold 3 and flat keyword-derived likelihood
expected: In the LLM_ENGINE=claude env, the path-choice the user came for should be model-reasoned (like the single-classification screening), or at minimum clearly flagged that the comparison itself is a keyword teaser distinct from the deep screening.
got: best-path/route.ts:57 calls recommendBestPath with no source override; the source param defaults 'mock' (best-path.ts:143) and the route comment states 'no model' (best-path/route.ts:17). Every program is scored by mockQualification with the same threshold 3, so likelihoods are a flat 38+8*qualifying heuristic. The finder is labelled 'Free · informational' (BestPathFinder.tsx:91) but does not distinguish itself from the deep model screening the user is about to reach.
suggested_acceptance: Either a model-backed best-path on the authenticated path, or a clear in-UI note that the comparison is a fast keyword pre-read and the per-program screening is the deep one.
l2_priority: Confirm live the best-path card renders identical structure for all 3 programs and that the only model reasoning happens after a program is picked. Related: OA-QV-02.

title: Hero and finder are honestly labelled keyword reads; the real model runs on the authenticated single-screening where her full paste is the prompt
expected: Mock outputs to a beneficiary should not masquerade as the deep model read.
got: InstantVerdict labels itself 'instant keyword read' (InstantVerdict.tsx:273) and source:'mock'; the preview routes never call the model (preview/route.ts:17,69). The authenticated /api/qualify runs the real model with buildQualifyPrompt fed the user's full pasted profile (qualification.ts:120-165), grounding 1/1 on that surface, and persists the chosen classification.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the honest labelling and the model-gated authenticated path.
l2_priority: Confirm the live single-screening output for Yuki names her real evidence (IGF nomination, 250k units, IGN/Polygon, GDC talk) on the O-1A pack.

title: Fabrication gate scans numbers only — a qualitative inflation (IGF nomination upgraded to a win) is not caught by fabricatedSpecifics
expected: The IGF nomination must never be inflated to a win in the draft (Yuki's #1 pet peeve); the safety net should catch an award-status inflation, not just numeric fabrication.
got: fabricatedSpecifics (adjudication-gates.ts:122) flags only money / percentages / 4-digit years / integers >=100 that appear in output but not input (specifics, :83-93). A semantic swap of 'nominated' -> 'won' carries no flaggable number, so the gate cannot catch it. Protection rests entirely on the prompt's 'do NOT invent awards' rule (drafting.ts:181) and on whether the word 'nomination' survived into the persisted criteria evidence text the draft argues from (the draft does not see the raw paste — accepted G1.3/PN-DRAFT-01).
suggested_acceptance: Either a qualitative-claim check (award-status: nomination vs win, shortlist vs winner) in the adjudication gates, or an L2-verified demonstration that the live draft preserves 'nomination' and never writes 'won/winner' for an IGF nomination.
l2_priority: Generate the live O-1A draft from a Yuki case whose evidence says 'IGF nomination' and assert the prose says nominated/finalist and never 'won the IGF' / 'IGF winner'; likewise assert no fabricated Steam unit count or review score.

title: Classification flows pack-correct end-to-end (qualify->case->criteria UI->draft->evidence buckets); strict citation discipline, injection-fencing, exhibit audit
expected: On Yuki's chosen O-1A path the whole pipeline must stay O-1A — no silent fallback-to-O-1A misfire and no pack drift.
got: Qualify persists the chosen classification; case detail reads stored.classification (page.tsx:78); DraftStudio + EvidenceVault + categorize all read the case classification and use criteriaNames(classification) (EvidenceVault.tsx:55, categorize/route.ts:58, draftOperation.ts:147-161). buildDraftPrompt enforces 'do not invent', no case law, and <<<CASE_DATA>>> injection-fencing (drafting.ts:174-208); auditCitations quarantines any (Exhibit N) with no on-file doc. Per-section regenerate passes sibling sections as continuity context (G1.1 shipped, drafting.ts:222-278).
suggested_acceptance: Protect the end-to-end classification threading and citation discipline.
l2_priority: Confirm live the O-1A draft sections map tech->Original contribution and IGN/Polygon->Press, and argue commercial success via Press/Original-contribution (not a non-existent O-1A commercial-success criterion).

title: Landing '/' is relentlessly O-1/'sciences' framed — under-signals arts/O-1B to a creative prospect on first read
expected: A cold game director who self-identifies as 'arts' should see, in the first read, that arts/O-1B is supported (so she doesn't bounce thinking it's a sciences-only tool).
got: Landing copy is O-1A/sciences-dominant: 'Your O-1 visa', 'the eight O-1 criteria', hero card 'O-1A · Sciences', audience line 'For founders · engineers · researchers · designers' (page.tsx:125,133,154,210). O-1B/arts is not surfaced on the landing. Mitigated downstream: /qualify explicitly compares O-1A/O-1B/EB-1A (qualify/page.tsx:30-38) and the FAQ names O-1B (faq/page.tsx:27) — but the first impression under-signals arts.
suggested_acceptance: The landing acknowledges arts/O-1B alongside O-1A (or the audience line includes creative/arts roles), so a game director/creative doesn't read the product as sciences-only.
l2_priority: Subjective/positioning — L2 to judge whether a creative prospect feels excluded by the landing before reaching /qualify.

title: Positioning and pricing consistent across landing/FAQ/billing; pricing canonical (can't drift); FAQ names O-1B and cites primary sources
expected: Unmistakable 'drafting tool, not a law firm; your attorney signs; not legal advice' with self-serve, non-drifting pricing.
got: Positioning line consistent on landing (page.tsx:254-256,156), FAQ (faq/page.tsx:27,31,43), and pricing copy; DISCLAIMER canonical (result.ts:37). Pricing driven from economy.ts BUNDLES (page.tsx:13,357) so landing/billing can't drift; /pricing 301s to /billing. FAQ cites 8 CFR 214.2(o)(3)(iii) / 204.5(h)(3) and links /validation.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the consistent positioning and canonical pricing source.

title: Share token carries no profile text, is DB-free, validates pack/status count, renders the correct pack with neutral-for-unscored, framed informational
expected: A shareable verdict safe to send a publisher/attorney — credible, leaking nothing private she didn't choose, carrying not-legal-advice framing.
got: encodeSnapshot puts only name/classification/likelihood/per-criterion statuses in the token — never the profile text (letters-patent.ts:21-29,70-76). decodeSnapshot rejects non-live programs and a status count that doesn't match the pack (:93-97). /c/[token] renders the correct pack via statusTone (unscored -> neutral, never green) and is framed 'Informational only · not legal advice · no account needed' (c/[token]/page.tsx:103-104,118-120). OG image likewise decodes from token only. The 'Certificate of Extraordinary Ability' ceremony framing is the known PN-QUAL-01/lf-share-02 polish, not re-reported.
suggested_acceptance: Protect the profile-free, pack-validated share token.
